independence_notes: Family-owned South Texas frac sand hauler. Self-describes as 'family-owned business...over 45 years of logistics excellence and 10+ years of experience hauling frac sand.' Fleet sized down from 200+ tractors to 41 between 2024-2025.

Family-owned and operated since 1969. Specialized/oversized/overweight hauling, rig moving, oilfield, project cargo. Fleet of 300+ trailers (exceeds power-unit range; flagged for separate verification of trucks vs trailers).

Family-owned since 1929. South Texas/border specialist (San Antonio, Del Rio, Laredo, Eagle Pass). Certified Hazmat carrier; bonded. Multi-trade FTL/LTL with hazmat capacity supports independent specialty hauling thesis.
Family-owned hot shot trucking serving Permian Basin oilfield, construction, manufacturing. Operating as owner-operator interstate carrier under DOT.
Family-owned since 1974 by Joe Burns. LTL/hot shot, forklift delivery; Houston-area + nationwide. 52+ years of operation aligns with long-tenured solo-owner thesis.
Founded 1977. DFW metroplex topsoil/aggregate/sand hauler. Family-owned status not on website (needs verification); no platform affiliation found.

Panhandle grain/feed/agricultural hauler. Above 50-truck target fleet size band (102 power units). Flag for cross-vertical inclusion only — likely too large for buyer thesis. Independent (no platform affiliation found).
Founded 1968 (hobby), incorporated 1973 by Dick Gripp. Originally cattle hauler, now refrigerated. Family-owned (Gripp family). TXTA member, Truckload Carriers Assoc, SmartWay.
Crude oil, condensate, fuel, LPG hauler. 42 trucks + 47 trailers; Eagle Ford + Permian operations with Three Rivers and Corpus Christi yards. Family-ownership not stated; flag for verification.

Permian Basin water transfer specialist. Founded 2023, recently acquired Hyflow Water Solutions (Dec 2025). Self-describes as 'generational water transfer service business that fathers can pass on to sons.' Brayden Woods CEO. NOTE: <3 years tenure—does not yet meet long-tenured acquisition profile.

Founded 1999 by Link Clifton. Eagle Ford + Permian crude hauling division since 2011. 200+ trucks (EXCLUDED: exceeds 10-50 fleet target). HQ Oklahoma City. Listed only for visibility — DROP from buyer-ready set.

East Texas frac sand hauler since 1988 — grew from 2 trucks to over 100 trucks (EXCEEDS 50-truck target). Founder Tim Ables still active per name. Service area: TX, LA, OK, AR. Independent (no platform affiliation found). Flag for cross-vertical only — exceeds size band.

Owner Mike Glover. Winch trucks, floats, dropdecks, equipment trailers for oilfield in Permian Basin + Concho Valley (Ozona, Midland, Odessa, Big Lake, San Angelo). Owner-operated suggests family-style operation.
Vacuum trucks, pump/kill trucks, hot oilers serving Permian Basin (Midland, Odessa, Andrews, Big Lake, Pecos). Family-ownership not stated.
'Locally owned and operated' oilfield transportation, Permian Basin focused. Pump trucks, hot oil trucks, acid pumping, water hauling, tank cleaning. Midland and Pecos service areas.
Family-owned 2006. Two divisions: aggregate (sand, gravel, rock, asphalt, scrap, non-haz waste) AND dry bulk/pneumatic (cement, fly ash). 'Passed on from generation to generation.'
Pneumatic dry-bulk specialist hauling cement, fly ash, lime, mineral fill. DBE certified. Late-model Kenworth/Freightliner fleet, in-house maintenance, 24/7 logistics. Family ownership not stated.
Family-owned and operated since 1992 (King family with deep livestock expertise). Cattle hauling from feedyards to Tyson Foods; mixed fleet of company drivers + owner-operators.
Family-owned since 2003. Fluid hauling, frac fluid, drilling mud, production water. Yards in Karnes City (Eagle Ford) and Hillsboro (Barnett).
Associated with Luckey Custom Feedlot Inc (est. 1982). Cattle, feed liquids, bulk grains, cotton seed hulls, DDG. 6 trucks/8 trailers/6 drivers — below 10-50 band; flag as smaller operator.
Family-owned hauling/trucking for select fill, sand, crushed concrete, topsoil, aggregates. HUB-certified. 'Over a decade of experience'.
Privately owned, 60+ years (founded 1965). Crude oil hauling + petroleum transport, San Antonio HQ. Long employee tenure noted.
Privately owned crude oil hauler, Permian Basin focused. Shell Goal Zero Awards 2017/2019/2020. 98 power units EXCEEDS 50-truck cap — flag for visibility only.
Family-owned since 2004. Crude oil + condensate hauling across TX. 15 power units; long employee retention emphasis. ISN + Veriforce safety members.
Family-owned and operated since 1982. Offices in Giddings, Madisonville, Caldwell, Tilden. 26 Hot Oil Units + frac water heater. Intrastate hazmat carrier.
Honeycutt family-founded (1967). Corporate HQ Cabool MO; TX terminal Stephenville. Bulk food-grade/milk leader nationwide. Out-of-state HQ — flag as borderline match for TX-domiciled filter.

Family-owned (C.D. Ballard, president). Acquired by Ballard family in 1980s and consolidated; daughters Tammy Ballard Acker (Ballard Drivers Inc) + Terri Monday active. Refrigerated milk transport since 1967. 29 power units in Sulphur Springs entity (broader Ballard Enterprises operates ~70-100 trucks across Hopkins + Erath counties).
Family-owned by Dennis & Julie Carroll, founded 2013. Note: HRL is primarily 3PL/warehousing + cross-dock + refrigerated, so trucking is secondary to storage. Flag for NAICS check (likely 484 + 493).

Founded May 2004 by Jaime A. Harris and Rogelio Gonzalez (Houston Ship Channel area). Flatbeds, single/double drops, stretch trailers, RGNs, vans, gooseneck, container. Specializes in oilfield/heavy equipment + petrochemical. Self-described as family-led with Joe Harris joining in 2005. 15 power units per SAFER (other refs say 30; flag for verification).
Family-owned 40+ years. Fluid + waste mgmt for O&G in TX, NM, OK. Big Country + Permian. 120 power units EXCEEDS 50 — flag as too large; include for cross-vertical only.
Founded 1978. 19 power units + 22 LPG tank trailers + 6 asphalt oil trailers. LPG + asphalt transport TX/OK/LA. 'Ahrens Brothers' name implies family-formed though website doesn't state explicitly.
Central TX (Dripping Springs HQ + San Angelo office). NGL/Y-grade/raw mix/propane hauling. 'Family-orientated' culture but ownership structure not stated.
Founded 1995 by Joe Pulido (former truck driver). Lubricants, waxes, chemicals across lower 48. Terminals in Houston + Beaumont. Asset-based (owns own equipment).
27 power units. Livestock, grain, feed, hay, dry bulk. Nearly two decades in Texas Panhandle. James Barrett associated. No platform affiliation; independent.

19 power units. General freight + milk hauling in Texas Panhandle dairy belt (Castro County). 1.47M annual mileage. Phone number is 559 (CA) area code — flag for ownership verification, may be CA-controlled.
20 power units, 4 employee drivers. East TX (Mt Enterprise). General freight, logs/lumber, oilfield equipment, water well. Family-ownership not yet verified.
49 power units (top of target range). Permian-based oilfield carrier: general freight, metal, machinery, oilfield, utility, construction. Independent (no platform affiliation found).

legal_name: West Texas Frac Sand Logistics LLC
independence_notes: 40 power units. Permian frac sand specialist + bottom drop + hot shot. West TX + SE NM. Family/independent status unverified.
14 power units. 'Built in Texas and proven in the Permian.' Sand + oilfield hauling. Seminole TX HQ. Family/independent inferred from messaging.
Family-owned with 70+ years industry experience. Crane services, heavy haul, dirt + oilfield construction. Midland, Odessa, Pecos, Hobbs (NM).

Cattle hauling since 1946. Schwertner family operation (founded by Bernard Schwertner & sons, originally Austrian immigrants 1877). Multi-generational. 11 power units; runs ~400K head/year through facility. Paul Casey contact.
9 power units, dual oilfield equipment + livestock authority. Intrastate. Family-ownership unverified; deep Hereford ag region.
36 power units, intrastate saltwater/fresh water hauler. West Central TX (Stephens County). Independent (no platform affiliation found). Family-ownership unverified.

Texas' oldest oilfield trucking & hauling company since 1895. Roots: Sour Lake (TX 1st major oil discovery). McDowell family acquired Dewey interests mid-1950s; 2nd + 3rd generation still active. Pipe-hauling leader 125+ years. 40 company tractors + 110 flatbeds + owner-operators.

Family-owned, founded by Brad and Kimila Pohlmeier October 2003. Regional cargo transport: liquid asphalt, crude oil, anhydrous ammonia, petrochemicals, milk/food-grade, fertilizers, dry bulk. Texas Panhandle hub.

Family-owned bulk fuel delivery (gasoline, diesel, oils, lubricants, DEF) across TX/OK/LA. 25 vehicles. NOTE: Primary line is fuel distribution to retailers — flag for NAICS verification (484220 vs 424720 fuel wholesale).
Family-owned + operated. '50 truck drivers in the family' delivering petroleum to 1000+ c-stores, big-box retailers, commercial accounts across TX. NOTE: Likely fuel wholesale → flag NAICS.
21 power units, interstate liquids/gases carrier. Houston ship channel area. HAZMAT-certified drivers, on-site fleet maintenance. Family-ownership unverified.
Family-owned since 1970 (2nd generation). 54+ years experience. Heavy machinery, industrial equipment, hot shot moves, air + ocean freight + trucking.
Family-owned since 1980 (founded by Luis C. Zamora). End-dump + dump truck aggregate hauling. DBE/MBE/SBE/HUB for TXDOT jobs. San Antonio metro.

All-pneumatic frac sand hauler, 100% owner-operator model. Fort Worth HQ. Service areas: STX, NTX, WTX, SETX, PA, OH. Independent — family-ownership not stated. NOTE: 100% owner-operator means low asset base → flag for buy-side fit.
Founded 1988. 7 power units SAFER (website claims 20+ units incl. dump trucks). HUB/DBE certified. Brazos Valley aggregate/heavy equipment + demolition. Below 10-truck band — flag.
Hot asphalt, hot liquids, crude/fuel oils. 159 power units (EXCEEDS 50 cap; flag for visibility only). Channelview/Houston HQ since 1997. Terminals in TX, OK, AR, TN.
Family-owned by Judy and Terry Loomis since 1993. Hotshot + 1 semi trailer. Very small operation — flag fleet size.
27 power units, 25 employees. Atascosa County (South TX/Eagle Ford). Sand, gravel, grain, feed, hay, construction materials. Private property interstate. Family-ownership unverified.

Founded 1987 by Jack Hearn Jr. (3rd-generation trucker). Started with 1 truck; now 55 power units (just above 50-truck cap — include with flag). Multi-service: livestock, refrigerated, dry bulk cement, dry bulk sand, flatbed.
Founded by BJ Oden (~18 years industry experience per website). Heavy equipment, oilfield, agricultural, construction. Permian-focused. 6 power units — below 10-truck band, flag for size.
Cotton/cotton byproducts hauling. 6 power units — below 10-truck band, flag for size. Intrastate non-haz.
Note: duplicate of earlier Petroleum Express entry — retain. 21 power units, interstate liquids/gases hauler at Houston Ship Channel.
Family-owned (Gary Garrison, owner). Hazmat tanker for liquids/gases. Texas Panhandle (Plainview). 51 power units — top of target range. Texas Food & Fuel Association member.

legal_name: Southeast Texas Timber & Trucking Co Inc
independence_notes: 29 years operating. East Texas timber/lumber hauler. Todd R Mayo, President. 2 power units — well below 10-truck cap, flag as small fleet.
19 trucks, hazmat-certified, interstate liquids/gases hauler. NOTE: SAFER record currently marked INACTIVE — flag for status verification before contact. Lubbock fuel hauling specialist.
Multi-commodity tanker hauler: crude, cryogenics (N2, O2, CO2, Ar), CNG, water, diesel, jet fuel. Operates 24/7 in Permian Basin and beyond. Address/founding year not on public site.
'Midland born' Permian oilfield carrier. Fleet: flatbeds, dry vans, step decks, RGN, vac tankers, hydrovac, hot shot. Pecos, Big Spring, Loving County service area. Family ownership not stated.
Frac sand + flatbed + water hauling, oilfield chemicals + acid. Founded/run by Benjamin Kail (oil & gas since 2011). Permian Basin focus. Family-ownership status unverified.

Self-described as 'Houston-based, family-owned logistics carrier'. Flatbed + intermodal + energy/construction/manufacturing. HazMat certified. Asset-based (owns trucks + specialized trailers). USDOT not on landing page — flag for verification.

Family-owned. 150+ local owner-operators (100% owner-operator model — flag for low-asset profile). Aggregate hauler: limestone, river rock, granite, sand, fill, recycled. Central TX (Austin, Bryan, Killeen, Round Rock, San Antonio, Temple, Waco).

Family-owned by Casey and Celeste Raitz. Stephenville HQ. Sand/gravel/aggregate hauling. 60+ employees. Fleet of Freightliner + Western Star. Specific USDOT, fleet size, founding year not on website — flag for verification.
Founded 1995. Heavy haul + RGN trucking, East Texas (Kilgore, Longview, Tyler, Henderson). Serves mining, energy, construction, logging. Family-ownership not explicitly stated.

Family-owned AND woman-owned. Refrigerated transport for perishables, dairy, fresh produce, flowers, pharma; general freight. Texas Panhandle service area (Dimmitt, Hereford, Muleshoe). NOTE: website mixes 'Weather Seal Insulation' branding — likely sister business, flag for verification.

Ben Burkholder, President. Texas-based (Lubbock + West TX roots). Crude oil + fluid hauling + field services. 129 power units (EXCEEDS 50 cap; flag for cross-vertical only). Sister company Salty Dawg Trucking.
